name: OWASP AISVS — AI Vendor Security Questionnaire
description: Due-diligence questionnaire for assessing a third-party AI vendor / tool against OWASP AISVS v1.0. Questions are vendor self-attestations, paraphrased from AISVS L1/L2 requirements (referenced by ID); they are not a guarantee of vendor AI safety. Based on OWASP AISVS v1.0 (CC-BY-SA-4.0). Source: https://github.com/OWASP/AISVS
attribution: Based on OWASP AISVS v1.0 — © OWASP Foundation, CC-BY-SA-4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/). Source: https://github.com/OWASP/AISVS. Questions are paraphrased references to AISVS requirement IDs, not verbatim requirement text.

questions.prompt: Which best describes the AI system being procured? (Used to decide which sections apply — RAG/embeddings and agentic sections only apply to those architectures.)
Do you maintain an inventory of all training-data sources and their origin/provenance? (AISVS C1.1.2, L2)
Do you protect training-data integrity in storage and transfer (e.g. checksums, access control)? (AISVS C1.1.3, L2)
Do you screen training pipelines for data poisoning? (AISVS C1.3.1, L2)
Do you remove disallowed or unlawful content from training data before training? (AISVS C1.3.4, L2)
Do you screen untrusted inputs for prompt-injection attempts before they reach the model? (AISVS C2.1.3, L1)
Do you enforce an instruction hierarchy that protects system instructions from user/tenant-supplied content? (AISVS C2.1.6, L2)
Do you score prompts for harmful content against defined thresholds? (AISVS C2.2.1, L1)
Do you maintain an inventory of deployed model artifacts and versions? (AISVS C3.1.1, L1)
Do you cryptographically sign model artifacts? (AISVS C3.1.2, L2)
Do you run automated safety and validation testing before deploying a model change? (AISVS C3.2.1, L1)
Do you stage model rollouts with an automated rollback path? (AISVS C3.3.1, L2)

sections.title: Access Control & Identity for AI (AISVS C5)

questions.prompt: Do you enforce default-deny authorization on AI resources and capabilities? (AISVS C5.2.1, L2)
Do you filter model outputs to block exposure of unauthorized data? (AISVS C5.2.4, L2)
Do you prevent cross-tenant interference and data leakage on shared AI infrastructure? (AISVS C5.3.1, L2)
Do you scan third-party models for malicious code before use? (AISVS C6.1.1, L1)
Do you obtain models only from approved, trusted sources? (AISVS C6.1.2, L1)
Can you provide a machine-readable AI bill of materials (AI BOM) for the system? (AISVS C6.2.1, L1)
Are third-party model artifacts integrity-verifiable (signatures/hashes)? (AISVS C6.1.3, L2)
Do you validate model outputs against a defined schema before returning them? (AISVS C7.1.1, L1)
Do you scan responses and block harmful content (output guardrails)? (AISVS C7.3.1, L1)
Do you estimate answer reliability/confidence and handle low-confidence outputs? (AISVS C7.2.1, L2)
For retrieval-grounded answers, do you include source attribution? (AISVS C7.4.1, L1)
Do you log AI interactions with sufficient context for investigation? (AISVS C12.1.1, L1)
Do you detect jailbreak and prompt-injection attempts at runtime? (AISVS C12.2.1, L1)
Do you detect and flag hallucinations in model outputs? (AISVS C12.3.2, L2)
Do you keep an audit log of security-critical AI actions with approver identity? (AISVS C12.4.2, L2)

sections.title: Memory, Embeddings & Vector DB — RAG vendors only (AISVS C8)

questions.prompt: Do vector identifiers enforce per-tenant uniqueness/isolation in the index? (AISVS C8.1.1, L1)
Do you mask sensitive fields before vectorization/embedding? (AISVS C8.2.1, L1)
Do you provide a memory/embedding reset (deletion) capability? (AISVS C8.3.2, L2)

sections.title: Orchestration & Agentic Security — agentic vendors only (AISVS C9)

questions.prompt: Do you enforce per-tool quotas and timeouts on agent actions? (AISVS C9.1.1, L1)
Do high-impact / privileged agent actions require human approval? (AISVS C9.2.1, L1)
Do you execute tools in least-privilege sandboxes? (AISVS C9.3.1, L1)
